summary: A solid sound‑board capture with punchy bass that delivers a scorching China > Rider transition, a standout “Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude” pairing and the rare 1988 ‘Saint’.
review: The show is generally praised for its high energy – especially the hot China > Rider segue, enthusiastic performances of Brown‑Eyed Women, Masterpiece and the one‑off 1988 Saint – while the recording, though not as crisp as top‑tier SBD tapes, offers a clear enough mix with strong low end.  A few listeners note muddiness in the China/Rider stretch and occasional lack of sparkle, but overall the performance outweighs the technical quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Eight detailed reviews average 4.1 ★, with most highlighting strong musical moments and only moderate recording complaints; the weighted analysis (70 % show, 30 % recording) supports a rating in the low‑to‑mid‑4‑star range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Big, punchy bass and decent overall balance; less crisp than premium SBD tapes with some muddiness on the China > Rider segment, but still listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid with a few extra Phil “bombs”, strong mid‑set energy, and a second set that shines thanks to the rare 1988 Saint, consistent vocal trade‑offs and an engaging Drums/Space segment.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed feedback – some say his playing was off, others praise his high‑energy vocals and firm harmonizing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for abundant bass “bombs” that some felt were excessive in the first set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for enthusiastic lead vocals on Masterpiece and overall vocal presence.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Hammond work is highlighted as a strong point, though one reviewer felt he was occasionally too busy during China > Rider.
band_member_comments.Drums: Jam/Drums segment out of He’s Gone mentioned as a highlight; the drum break is described as normal‑sized and solid.
